workingPrinciple: Two antiparallel Silicon Controlled Rectifier (SCR) structures share a gate; a trigger pulse turns the device on in whichever direction current is flowing, latching until current crosses zero.
steps: Alternating Current (AC) voltage applied across MT1-MT2
At current zero it turns off
Next half-cycle triggers again as needed
microscopicExplanation: The Triode for Alternating Current (TRIAC) is effectively two SCRs in inverse parallel; the gate triggers the conducting quadrant, and the device commutates off at each AC zero-crossing.
